note: Quieter run than 5/1 (8 leads). Deduped against 5/1 pr-freelance batch (Siege Media, Mission North, BVNK, Coinbase Base, Atticus, Cloudbeds, FactSet, Robinhood), 4/30 batch (5WPR, HockeyStack, Crypto.com, Courier Health, Forward Financing), 4/27 targeted batch (SerpApi, PayNearMe, Paddle, Loti AI), and pr-freelance leads inside the 4/29-5/1 daily scrapes. Only 3 credible new openings cleared the 55+ scoring threshold. PR hiring market reverted to quieter baseline after last week's bunched activity. Tide (UK) and Satispay (Italy) surfaced but scored below threshold and were excluded. Recommended priority order: Offchain Labs (best signal — overflow door explicit in JD, requires crypto fluency), then GlossGenius (3-month-stale search = real capacity gap), then Upstart (strongest sector fit but lowest conversion probability — single-shot only).

leads.name: Offchain Labs
leads.industry: Web3 / Ethereum L2 — builds the Arbitrum stack powering Arbitrum One, the most widely adopted Ethereum scaling solution
leads.fundingStage: $124M raised (Lightspeed, Polychain, Pantera, Ribbit) — late-stage scale-up

contacts.name: Steven Goldfeder
contacts.title: Co-founder & CEO, Offchain Labs — likely sponsor of senior comms hire

contacts.name: Offchain Labs External PR Agency (incumbent)
contacts.title: Identify via PRNewswire / Arbitrum Foundation press release boilerplate — pitch on overflow capacity

leads.notes: Combined 71 (B grade) — top lead of the week. The single most actionable phrase across all 3 leads is in this JD: 'oversee external PR agencies and partners globally.' Two doors open: (1) the new hire's first 90 days when the agency roster gets re-evaluated, (2) the interim gap when the existing agency is carrying everything under load. Two-track outreach: direct LinkedIn note to CEO Steven Goldfeder positioning Mark for fintech-adjacent / institutional Web3 narrative work, AND identify the incumbent PR agency (search PRNewswire and Arbitrum Foundation press releases for boilerplate) and pitch on overflow. CRITICAL RISK: same Web3 fluency bar as Robinhood Crypto and Coinbase Base — Mark must be able to discuss rollups, sequencers, and Arbitrum vs Optimism in a 5-minute conversation. If not, this is a hard NO regardless of fit.

leads.name: GlossGenius
leads.industry: Vertical SaaS for beauty pros — booking, payments, marketing for salons and independent beauty professionals

contacts.name: GlossGenius CMO / VP Marketing
contacts.title: Comms-function owner / hiring manager for Director, PR & Communications role

leads.notes: Combined 68 (B grade). The Director, PR & Communications role has been continuously open and posted since Feb 6, 2026 — roughly 3 months on the market. A search this stale signals one of three things, all pitchable: (1) high hiring bar, no candidate yet → real capacity gap; (2) internal priority shifted but role still being maintained → narrative work being deferred; (3) outside agency or freelancer is covering in the interim → competitor exists but pricing/scope re-evaluation likely. In any scenario, an interim 60-90 day retainer covering 'narrative, exec thought leadership, media relationships' is well within Mark's wheelhouse. Pitch angle: single LinkedIn note to GlossGenius CMO. Frame: 'You've had this Director search open ~3 months. While you keep searching for the right permanent hire, I can carry the media-relations and exec thought-leadership workstreams on a 60-day interim, no long-term commitment, hand off cleanly when you find your Director.' Reasonable retainer: $10-15K/month. RISK: sector adjacency is meaningful — SMB / consumer beauty press is different from B2B fintech press. Mark should be honest about his B2B/finance-tilted Rolodex but lean on workstream overlap (narrative architecture, exec thought leadership) which is sector-agnostic.

leads.name: Upstart
leads.industry: Consumer lending fintech — AI-driven credit underwriting platform; partners with banks and credit unions
leads.location: Multi-city: Columbus OH, Austin TX, Bay Area, NYC (opening Summer 2026); remote-flexible

contacts.name: Upstart Communications Leadership
contacts.title: VP Communications / Chief Communications Officer (hiring manager for Director Corporate Comms)

leads.notes: Combined 64 (C grade). Most sweet-spot lead of this batch sectorally — lending fintech, AI underwriting, and macroeconomic/regulatory narrative are all Mark's home turf. JD literally calls out 'translate complex topics, including lending, credit, AI, regulation, and macroeconomic trends, into clear and compelling stories' — that's essentially Mark's positioning sentence. CONSTRAINT (same as FactSet/Robinhood last week): public company with established corporate comms function, IR overlay, and almost certainly a tier-1 PR agency on retainer. No first-PR-hire dynamic. Realistic angles: (1) interim coverage if Director search stretches past 90 days, (2) specialized B2B/lender-customer narrative workstream existing function may not be staffed for. ONE well-targeted email to hiring manager (likely VP Comms or CCO). Lead with sector credibility — name a recent Upstart story or AI-credit-underwriting trade angle. Don't multi-touch. Single shot only. Probability of fractional engagement is genuinely low but comp tier and budget make even a small interim retainer ($15-20K/month for 90 days) plausible if timing aligns with executive vacuum.
